name: Peano Arithmetic & Gödel — Part 7: First Incompleteness Theorem
description: Gödel's First Incompleteness Theorem: Any consistent, recursively axiomatized theory extending Robinson arithmetic is incomplete. Proof via diagonal lemma: construct sentence G such that G ↔ ¬Prov(#G).
mermaid:
graph TD
    RefRep["Ref: Representability (Part 5)"]
    DefProv["Def: Provability predicate Prov(y) = ∃m Prf(m,y)"]
    LemProvRep["Lem: Prov is Σ₁ and representable"]
    DefDiag["Def: Diagonal function: diag(n) = #(φ(n̄) when #φ = n"]
    LemDiagRep["Lem: diag is representable in PA"]
    LemDiagonal["Lem: Diagonal Lemma: for any ψ(x), ∃ sentence G with PA ⊢ G ↔ ψ(#Ḡ)"]
    DefG["Def: Gödel sentence G: PA ⊢ G ↔ ¬Prov(#Ḡ)"]
    LemGUnprov["Lem: If PA consistent, PA ⊬ G"]
    LemGNegUnprov["Lem: If PA ω-consistent, PA ⊬ ¬G"]
    ThmFirstIncomp["Thm: First Incompleteness: consistent PA is incomplete"]
    CorRosser["Cor: Rosser: ω-consistency not needed; use Rosser trick"]
    RefRep --> DefProv
    RefRep --> LemProvRep
    LemProvRep --> DefProv
    RefRep --> DefDiag
    RefRep --> LemDiagRep
    DefDiag --> LemDiagRep
    LemDiagRep --> LemDiagonal
    LemProvRep --> LemDiagonal
    LemDiagonal --> DefG
    DefG --> LemGUnprov
    DefG --> LemGNegUnprov
    LemGUnprov --> ThmFirstIncomp
    LemGNegUnprov --> ThmFirstIncomp
    ThmFirstIncomp --> CorRosser
    classDef definition fill:#b197fc,color:#fff,stroke:#9775fa
    classDef lemma fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff,stroke:#4dabf7
    classDef theorem fill:#51cf66,color:#fff,stroke:#40c057
    classDef corollary fill:#1abc9c,color:#fff,stroke:#16a085
    classDef ref fill:#bdc3c7,color:#333,stroke:#95a5a6
    class RefRep ref
    class DefProv,DefDiag,DefG definition
    class LemProvRep,LemDiagRep,LemDiagonal,LemGUnprov,LemGNegUnprov lemma
    class ThmFirstIncomp theorem
    class CorRosser corollary
